project.name: TradeLab
project.summary: TradeLab is a multi-asset crypto paper-trading platform for demo execution, strategy experimentation, and trading workflow validation.

runtime_components: GitHub release and image publication pipeline
key_workflows: market-data fetch with bounded stale fallback
structured backend request and service logging

constraints.delivery_model: Feature branches -> PR CI -> merge to master -> release
maintenance_expectations: Keep tests aligned with behavior changes.
Adjust structured logging when backend control flow changes.
Update documentation and docs/ai-metadata.json when repository structure or workflows materially change.
Update GitHub Actions when quality gates or release flow need to evolve.

disclaimers.financial: TradeLab does not provide financial advice or investment recommendations.
disclaimers.ai_assistance: The repository includes AI-assisted code and documentation.

github_actions_sequence.action: Squash merge pull request into master

conditions: pull request was merged when triggered from pull_request_target
jobs_order: Build backend binaries, Build frontend artifact, Publish backend image, and Publish frontend image

observability: Request lifecycle logging in the HTTP layer
Service-level logging for sessions, market data, orders, portfolio, and history

user_workflows: Open the app and create a demo session automatically.
Inspect the default XRP/USDT reference market and chart feed state.
Switch intervals or markets without clearing portfolio panels.
Review balances, positions, orders, and activity after execution.

contribution_expectations: Behavior changes should include tests when practical.
Documentation should stay aligned with product and process changes.
docs/ai-metadata.json should be updated when public repository structure changes.
Security issues should not be reported through public issues.
public_repo_standards.merge_model: pull request review plus squash merge to master

change_management.authoritative_purpose: Primary dependency map for repo-facing follow-up work. Review this section whenever code, docs, workflows, GitHub surface, screenshots, or release behavior change.

ui_visual_change.description: Visible UI styling, layout, component composition, or screen-level presentation changed materially.
conditional_followups: e2e_tests if the visible change affects the main user path
developer_docs if screenshot generation or frontend conventions changed
validation_expectations: Regenerate screenshots if the visible output changed materially
Review frontend unit coverage for affected components

ui_user_flow_change.description: User-facing behavior or the sequence of steps inside the product changed.
conditional_followups: visual_docs if the changed flow is shown in screenshots
frontend_tests if local UI state behavior changed
validation_expectations: Update or add E2E coverage for the changed path

frontend_data_flow_change.description: Frontend API integration, state loading, caching, or client-side data behavior changed.
conditional_followups: user_docs if visible behavior changed
api_docs if the frontend consumer contract changed
validation_expectations: Review unit coverage around state transitions
Review E2E impact on core journeys

api_surface_change.description: Routes, request/response shapes, auth expectations, or public API capabilities changed.
conditional_followups: frontend_tests if the frontend consumes the API
e2e_tests if the API affects a user-visible path
planning_docs if the API reflects a new product capability
validation_expectations: Update public or contributor-facing API references
Add or adjust backend test coverage

backend_behavior_change.description: Business logic, logging, service behavior, authorization rules, or execution semantics changed.
conditional_followups: operator_docs if runtime behavior changed
api_docs if external behavior changed
e2e_tests if the behavior is user-visible
validation_expectations: Review unit or integration test impact

domain_or_data_model_change.description: Core entities, persistence shape, domain language, or storage-level concepts changed.
conditional_followups: api_docs if the change is externally visible
operator_docs if deployment or migration behavior changed
validation_expectations: Review API and workflow docs for terminology drift

configuration_change.description: Environment variables, runtime settings, local setup, or deployment configuration expectations changed.
conditional_followups: workflow_files if CI or release configuration changed
deployment_assets if runtime manifests changed
validation_expectations: Review local and deployment setup instructions
Review metadata for new or changed configuration keys

deployment_change.description: Kubernetes, container, infra packaging, or runtime deployment shape changed.
conditional_followups: workflow_files if CI/release packaging changed
developer_docs if local operator workflow changed
validation_expectations: Review release docs and rollout checklist

release_flow_change.description: Release trigger logic, artifact creation, image publication, or GitHub release behavior changed.
conditional_followups: operator_docs if deployment expectations changed
validation_expectations: Review README quality and delivery section

github_process_change.description: Contribution flow, templates, labels, community-health docs, or repository governance changed.
conditional_followups: public_docs if the public repo presentation changed
release_and_delivery_docs if workflow behavior changed

documentation_structure_change.description: Docs were added, moved, renamed, or materially restructured.
conditional_followups: operator_docs if operational docs moved
planning_docs if roadmap or product docs changed

screenshots_or_visual_assets_change.description: Documentation screenshots, social preview assets, or visual documentation generators changed.
conditional_followups: developer_docs if screenshot generation workflow changed

testing_strategy_change.description: The expected test mix, validation flow, or quality-gate strategy changed.
conditional_followups: community_health_docs if contributor expectations changed
public_docs if the repository quality posture changed materially

required_actions: Review and regenerate screenshots if the visible output changed materially
Review frontend unit tests and E2E coverage
Update API-facing docs and relevant public docs
Review frontend consumers and tests if applicable
Update docs/ai-metadata.json when the public repo contract changed
Review README quality and delivery section
Review developer/contributing guidance if local expectations changed

examples.scenario: Add a new GitHub template or process
required_actions: Update CONTRIBUTING.md or other community-health docs as needed
